rows.label: Basic vs. From Scratch ingredient mode
Import recipes from a web link

competitors.name: Mealime
competitors.summary: Mealime is a well-known meal planning app built around a curated library of quick weeknight recipes and automatically generated shopping lists.

switch_reasons.title: Cook what you already have
switch_reasons.body: Mealime plans around its own recipe catalogue. FoodieFlow's Smart Assistant looks at what's already in your kitchen first, so you buy less and waste less.

switch_reasons.title: A Smart Meal Assistant
switch_reasons.body: Tell FoodieFlow what you're craving in plain language and it returns tailored ideas in seconds — not just a fixed set of curated recipes to browse.

switch_reasons.title: Bring your own meals
switch_reasons.body: FoodieFlow lets you save any meal you love into a personal library and plans around it, rather than limiting you to a pre-built catalogue.

competitor_strengths: A large, professionally curated catalogue of fast recipes

faqs.q: Is FoodieFlow a good alternative to Mealime?
faqs.a: Yes. FoodieFlow covers the same core jobs as Mealime — weekly planning and automatic shopping lists — and adds a Smart Assistant plus pantry-aware suggestions that plan around the food you already own.

faqs.q: Does FoodieFlow have automatic shopping lists like Mealime?
faqs.a: Yes. Every ingredient from your weekly plan is pulled together and sorted by grocery aisle automatically, so you can shop without doubling back.
FoodieFlow is free to download and use. FoodieFlow Pro unlocks unlimited Smart Assistant suggestions and full nutrition data from £3.99/month.

competitors.name: Paprika
competitors.summary: Paprika is a long-standing recipe manager focused on clipping recipes from around the web, organising them, and building grocery lists — usually as a one-time purchase per platform.

switch_reasons.title: Ideas, not just storage
switch_reasons.body: Paprika is excellent at storing recipes you find yourself. FoodieFlow actively generates new meal ideas for you based on your preferences and pantry — so you never start from a blank week.

switch_reasons.title: Preferences that actually filter
switch_reasons.body: FoodieFlow remembers your allergies, restrictions and dislikes and filters every suggestion around them automatically. Paprika has no preference engine of its own.

switch_reasons.title: Built-in nutrition
switch_reasons.body: FoodieFlow shows calorie and macro estimates for every meal, with full breakdowns on Pro — no manual entry required.

competitor_strengths: Best-in-class recipe clipping from any website
One-time purchase with no ongoing subscription
Desktop apps for Mac and Windows

faqs.q: Is FoodieFlow a good alternative to Paprika?
faqs.a: It depends on what you want. Paprika is a recipe organiser you fill yourself. FoodieFlow is a meal-planning assistant that generates ideas, learns your dietary preferences, and plans around your pantry — a better fit if you want the app to do the thinking.

faqs.q: Does FoodieFlow let me save my own recipes like Paprika?
faqs.a: Yes. You can save any meal you love into your personal Meal Library, and the Smart Assistant uses it to suggest meals that suit your household.
FoodieFlow is free to download, with an optional Pro subscription from £3.99/month that unlocks unlimited Smart Assistant suggestions and full nutrition data.

competitors.name: Plan to Eat
competitors.summary: Plan to Eat is a subscription meal planner centred on importing recipes from the web, dragging them onto a calendar, and turning the plan into a shopping list.

switch_reasons.title: No blank calendar to fill
switch_reasons.body: Plan to Eat asks you to bring every recipe yourself. FoodieFlow's Smart Assistant fills the gaps with personalised ideas so you're never staring at an empty week.

switch_reasons.title: Pantry-first planning
switch_reasons.body: FoodieFlow prioritises meals you can make with what you already have and leaves those items off your shopping list, cutting waste and spend.

switch_reasons.title: Preferences and allergies built in
switch_reasons.body: Set your dietary needs once and every FoodieFlow suggestion respects them — no manual filtering.

competitor_strengths: Powerful recipe importer for web recipes
Loyal community and shared recipe features

faqs.q: Is FoodieFlow a good alternative to Plan to Eat?
faqs.a: Yes, especially if you want help deciding what to cook. Plan to Eat organises recipes you supply; FoodieFlow generates ideas for you, plans around your pantry, and respects your dietary preferences automatically.

faqs.q: Can FoodieFlow build a shopping list from my week like Plan to Eat?
faqs.a: Yes. Your shopping list is generated automatically from your weekly plan and grouped by aisle.
FoodieFlow is free to download. Pro is available from £3.99/month for unlimited Smart Assistant suggestions and full nutrition data.

competitors.name: Samsung Food
competitors.summary: Samsung Food (formerly Whisk) is a free recipe-saving and meal-planning platform with social and AI features, tied into Samsung's wider ecosystem.

switch_reasons.title: Focused, not sprawling
switch_reasons.body: Samsung Food blends a social recipe network, appliance integrations and planning. FoodieFlow does one thing well: get you from fridge to table with the least effort.

switch_reasons.title: Independent and privacy-minded
switch_reasons.body: FoodieFlow is an independent product — your meals, plans and preferences stay private and aren't tied to a larger hardware ecosystem or ad network.

switch_reasons.title: Pantry-first suggestions
switch_reasons.body: FoodieFlow prioritises recipes you can cook right now from what you already have, then trims those items from your shopping list.

competitor_strengths: Completely free with a huge recipe community
Integrations with Samsung appliances and the wider ecosystem

faqs.q: Is FoodieFlow a good alternative to Samsung Food?
faqs.a: Yes. Both offer smart, assisted planning and automatic shopping lists. FoodieFlow is a focused, independent app centred on quick weekly planning and pantry-aware suggestions, without the social network or ecosystem lock-in.

faqs.q: Does FoodieFlow have a smart meal assistant like Samsung Food?
faqs.a: Yes. The Smart Assistant generates personalised meal ideas from your preferences and pantry in seconds.
FoodieFlow is free to download, with an optional Pro plan from £3.99/month for unlimited Smart Assistant suggestions and full nutrition data.

competitors.name: AnyList
competitors.summary: AnyList is best known as a shared grocery list app with recipe storage and optional meal planning on its premium tier.

switch_reasons.title: Decides what to cook, not just what to buy
switch_reasons.body: AnyList is a superb list. FoodieFlow starts a step earlier — generating the meal plan itself — then produces the aisle-sorted list from it automatically.

switch_reasons.title: Smart suggestions and dietary profiles
switch_reasons.body: FoodieFlow suggests meals tailored to your tastes, allergies and pantry. AnyList has no suggestion engine or dietary filtering.

switch_reasons.title: Nutrition at a glance
switch_reasons.body: See calories and macros for every planned meal, with full breakdowns on Pro.

competitor_strengths: Excellent real-time shared and synced grocery lists
Great for households already coordinating shopping

faqs.q: Is FoodieFlow a good alternative to AnyList?
faqs.a: If you mainly want a shared grocery list, AnyList is hard to beat. If you want the app to plan your meals and then produce the list, FoodieFlow adds smart suggestions, dietary profiles and pantry-aware planning on top of an automatic list.

faqs.q: Does FoodieFlow create shopping lists automatically?
faqs.a: Yes. FoodieFlow builds your list from your weekly plan and sorts it by grocery aisle.
It's free to download, with Pro from £3.99/month for unlimited Smart Assistant suggestions and full nutrition data.

competitors.name: eMeals
competitors.summary: eMeals is a subscription service that delivers ready-made weekly meal plans by category, with shopping lists and grocery-delivery integrations.

switch_reasons.title: Your meals, your way
switch_reasons.body: eMeals gives you fixed, pre-built plans to follow. FoodieFlow builds plans around your tastes, your saved meals and what's in your kitchen — fully personalised, fully editable.

switch_reasons.title: Free to start
switch_reasons.body: FoodieFlow is free to download and plan with. eMeals is subscription-only from the outset.

switch_reasons.title: A meal library that grows with you
switch_reasons.body: Save any meal you love and FoodieFlow keeps suggesting more like it. eMeals centres on its own rotating plans rather than your collection.

competitor_strengths: Good for people who don't want to make any decisions

faqs.q: Is FoodieFlow a good alternative to eMeals?
faqs.a: Yes, if you want control and personalisation. eMeals hands you fixed plans to follow; FoodieFlow generates flexible plans around your preferences, saved meals and pantry, and is free to start.

faqs.q: Does FoodieFlow make plans for me like eMeals?
faqs.a: Yes — the Smart Assistant generates personalised meal ideas and you drop them into your weekly planner. Unlike eMeals, every plan is fully editable and built around your own tastes.
FoodieFlow is free to download and use. Pro is optional from £3.99/month. Pricing for other services varies, so check current rates before deciding.
